steps.title: Interpret 150% more as a growth factor
steps.content: If the population increases by 150%, the new population equals the old population plus 150% of the old population. If there are $N$ squirrels, the new count is $N + 1.5N = 2.5N$. Therefore, each year the population is multiplied by a factor of 2.5, which gives an exponential model: $n = a(2.5)^t$, where $a$ is the population when $t = 0$ (end of 2015).

steps.title: Use the given data point to find the initial value
steps.content:
The end of 2016 is $t = 1$ year after the end of 2015. We are told that $n = 180$ at this time. Substituting into our model:
$$180 = a(2.5)^1$$
$$180 = 2.5a$$
$$a = \frac{180}{2.5} = 72$$

steps.title: Write and identify the equation
steps.content: With $a = 72$, the equation becomes $n = 72(2.5)^t$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
